data.title: As an operator, deleted tickets are removed from Jira when the outbound bridge processes them, and the inbound bridge never overwrites a locally deleted ticket
data.description:
Story: Wire the Jira bridge to call acli delete when a ticket reaches deleted status, and add an inbound guard so syncing from Jira cannot resurrect a locally deleted ticket.

Done Definitions:
- acli-integration.py has a delete_issue(jira_key) method that runs: acli jira workitem delete --key <jira_key>; 404 response is treated as success (idempotent); 403 response writes a BRIDGE_ALERT event and skips deletion without crashing
- bridge-outbound.py STATUS handler intercepts compiled_status == 'deleted' at the TOP of the handler, before the generic update_issue call, and routes to delete_issue instead
- If jira_key is absent (ticket was never synced), the STATUS handler skips Jira deletion silently with a debug log entry
- bridge-outbound.py does NOT attempt to transition the Jira issue to a 'Deleted' workflow state -- only the ACLI delete call is made
- bridge-inbound.py skips writing any events for tickets where local compiled state has status == 'deleted' or archived == True; log entry emitted at debug level when skip occurs
- SYNC file (bridge-outbound.py) survives the deletion cycle -- the sync record is retained so future syncs do not re-create the Jira issue
- Integration tests (with acli mocked): (a) 200 delete success path, (b) 404 idempotent path, (c) 403 BRIDGE_ALERT path, (d) missing jira_key skip path, (e) inbound guard skips deleted ticket

Considerations:
- [Architecture] The bridge intercept must happen before _LOCAL_STATUS_TO_JIRA lookup -- the mapping has no 'deleted' key, so a missed intercept would attempt to transition to an undefined Jira state and raise an API error
- [Reliability] 403 BRIDGE_ALERT must not block the outbound bridge from processing other events in the same batch; log the alert and continue
- [Reliability] The inbound guard protects against resurrection when Jira deletion is delayed or fails -- local deleted status is the authoritative record and must not be overwritten by inbound sync
- [Adversarial finding] If deletion succeeds but the process crashes before the SYNC file is updated, a subsequent sync could re-create the Jira issue; consider whether the SYNC entry should be tombstoned rather than removed
